### Post by WangWeiLin on 2006-01-13
Who knows the Problem? I am running Kubuntu on a Samsung X10 with a Nvidia GF 5200 go and cannot get it to work proper. I can compile driver from the page or download them with apt-get but after the next restart they don't work anymore and I have to change the /etc/X11/xorg.conf to have my X running again... can u help me?

My ==>  /etc/X11/xorg.conf
> 
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

### Post by glacierre on 2006-01-13
Does X say that it can't find /dev/nvidia0 ? 

I have that problem, but it happens very few times so I can't find a solution (more that 10 days since last time). Try "modprobe nvidia", that worked for me... for the moment.

---

### Post by glacierre on 2006-01-13
Does X say that it can't find /dev/nvidia0 ? 

I have that problem, but it happens very few times so I can't find a solution (more that 10 days since last time). Try "modprobe nvidia", that worked for me... for the moment.

Also put "nvidia" in your /etc/modules file.

P.S. I'm sorry for the duped post.

---

### Post by Freddy on 2006-01-13
Did you install the restricted modules for your kernel and enabled your driver?   /// Freddan

---

### Post by mo_x on 2006-01-14
If you install the driver from the nvidia website you first have to remove the Ubuntu packages with --purge option and then reinstall the driver.
sudo apt-get remove --purge nvidia-glx

---

### Post by WangWeiLin on 2006-01-14
